title: Iran–USA: escalation paths in the Middle East 2023–2026

scenario.question: What are the most probable escalation paths between Iran and the United States over a 3-year horizon from 2023, and under what conditions does limited proxy conflict transition to direct military exchange?

assumptions: Baseline anchored on 2023 state: JCPOA negotiations stalled, full US sanctions regime active.
Iran nuclear enrichment at ~90% threshold as of 2023 Q3 (IAEA reports).
October 7 2023 Hamas attack treated as exogenous shock raising Israel conflict_level and Iran proxy activation.
Strait of Hormuz remains open at scenario start; maritime shock is a contingent path, not a baseline.
Saudi Arabia modelled as a cautious balancer: will not co-belligerent unless directly attacked.
Iraq is a passive arena actor: hosts Iranian proxy networks, US bases, and absorbs spillover.
The GIM15 2023 calibration state is the prior for all moderate outcomes.
Tail states (direct_strike_exchange, broad_regional_escalation) are preserved in the distribution when stress channels compound.

constraints: Cannot initiate overt conventional attack on US forces without proxy-escalation ladder preceding it.
Nuclear breakout triggers automatic broad_regional_escalation risk class activation.
Regime survival objective dominates all others under internal_destabilization conditions.
Domestic political cost penalty activates after year 2 if conflict intensity is sustained.
targeted_strike requires prior partial_mobilization signal or direct attack on US assets.
Cannot impose additional sanctions beyond current ceiling without new UNSC resolution.
Acts independently of USA on Iran nuclear timeline; will not wait indefinitely for diplomacy.
targeted_strike on Iran nuclear sites is available without US precondition.
Multi-front constraint: if active_war flag set with Gaza/Lebanon, partial_mobilization budget consumed.
Maritime chokepoint crisis (Hormuz closure) causes global oil shock: energy_dependence channel activates for all non-Middle-East agents in world state.
broad_regional_escalation requires at least two of: direct_strike_exchange active, Saudi Arabia conflict_level > 0.60, Iraq regime_crisis active.
negotiated_deescalation path requires backchannel_offer from at least one side AND accept_mediation response within same horizon year.
